threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the bus is a reliable option to get anywhere on time, bless you. The 38 is late more often than not, mate.
Last night on the 196, a guy serenaded the bus with a ukulele. We all pretended to ignore it, but I swear even the driver smirked.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I heard a full-blown techno rave from my balcony at 2 AM, while a street cat lounged in the sun on the windowsill. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a döner at 3 AM while a spontaneous techno party rages in the park. It's a vibe that just hits different.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re local if they have an opinion on which taqueria has the best lengua—don’t even bring up La Taqueria around folks from El Farolito.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy at Civic Center negotiating with a pigeon over a dropped French fry. Muni’s delayed, but the locals keep their priorities straight.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is just a pretty face, but they miss the late-night vermut scene where locals gather in hidden bars, sharing stories.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still insist on waiting for the tram at the stop even when the bus is just a few steps away; locals know to cut through the park.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: Mentioning how you miss the 'soul' of your old city while sipping a latte at an independent cafe—it’s the quickest way to spot a newcomer.
Last week on the N37, a guy played the sax while we all pretended not to listen. Perfectly in tune with the rumbles of the bus beneath.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I realized my local Späti also sells fresh flowers and homemade schnapps—only in Neukölln, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can find an open-air cinema under the stars in a former bus depot in Kreuzberg every summer. It's the quirkiest vibe with a local community feel.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they casually navigate around the Civic Center dog delays like it's just another Tuesday.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy singing opera on the 49 last week—full performance. Only in SF will a packed bus turn into a spontaneous concert.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is just for bars, but the real charm is its small bakeries and fruit shops tucked away on side streets. They miss the pulse.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by the way they still hesitate at traffic lights, waiting for the green man, while locals dart across without a second thought.
